steps.title: Substitute and set up the equation
Square both sides and form the quadratic
steps.content: Square both sides: $$53p + 110 = p^2$$ Rearrange to standard form: $$p^2 - 53p - 110 = 0$$

steps.title: Factor and identify the valid solution
steps.content: Find two numbers that multiply to $-110$ and sum to $-53$: $-55$ and \$2$. The quadratic factors as $(p - 55)(p + 2) = 0$, giving $p = 55$ or $p = -2$. Since the original equation $\sqrt{53p + 110} = p$ requires $p \geq 0$ (square roots output non-negative values), only $p = 55$ is valid.
